content: The existing Self-Refine framework for unassisted iterative output improvement can be meaningfully enhanced by integrating multiple inconsistent self-perspectives from LLM self-reflection mechanisms, creating a two-layer self-improvement system where the outer layer runs iterative refinement and the inner layer generates contrasting self-critiques from diverse solving perspectives at each step. This hybrid system retains Self-Refine's core advantage of requiring no external supervision or training, while addressing its limitation of getting stuck in narrow local feedback loops from a single initial perspective.
